features.description: ALBUM RESTRUCTURE + RECATEGORIZE: Replace the current 9-album structure with 11 JTBD albums. Read content/songs/ENGINEER-ONBOARD-PROMPT.md for the full album table. Create these album definitions in lib/albums.ts: the-craft, growth-engine (exists), by-the-numbers, the-weight, career-moves (exists), electric-dreams, the-startup-life (exists), the-talent-search, the-interview, deep-work, the-human-side (exists). Remove old albums that have no tracks left (founders, product, builders, the-operators, frameworks). Then recategorize these 16 existing tracks by changing their albumSlug and moving them to the correct track array: Do Things That Don't Scale → the-startup-life, Product-Market Fit (You'll Know) → the-startup-life, The Maze → the-startup-life, Taste → the-craft, Eleven Stars → the-craft, Talk to Users → the-craft, Ship It → the-craft, Vibe Coding → electric-dreams, Don't Give 'Em Your Number → the-interview, Fire with Grace → the-weight, When Enough Is Enough → the-human-side, Trace It Back → the-craft, The Disease → the-craft, Test Everything → growth-engine, What's the Job → the-craft, Good Enough → deep-work. Each new album needs: slug, title, subtitle (short tagline), description (1-2 sentences), accentColor (pick distinct hex colors), coverImage (empty string for now), tracks array. Run pnpm build to verify. DONE: all 11 albums defined, all 16 tracks recategorized, pnpm build passes. DO NOT touch: track lyrics, annotations, audio files, or cover images. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, all tracks still render.
steps: Create new track arrays for new albums (the-craft, by-the-numbers, the-weight, electric-dreams, the-talent-search, the-interview, deep-work)
Move existing track objects from old arrays to new arrays based on recategorization table
Update albumSlug on each moved track
Remove empty old album arrays (founders, product, builders, the-operators, frameworks)
Create album definition objects for all 11 albums in the albums export

features.description: BATCH 2 REPLACEMENT — dont-break-the-streak + im-in-the-room: These tracks already exist in lib/albums.ts but need new MP3s, new covers, and new annotations. For each track: (1) Extract cover art: ffmpeg -i content/songs/batch2/new-mp3s/[file].mp3 -an -vcodec copy public/covers/[slug].jpg (2) Copy MP3: cp content/songs/batch2/new-mp3s/[file].mp3 public/audio/[slug].mp3 (3) Read lyrics from content/songs/batch2/lyrics/01-duolingo-streak.md (for dont-break-the-streak) and content/songs/batch2/lyrics/02-founder-mode.md (for im-in-the-room) (4) Update the existing track objects in lib/albums.ts — make sure albumSlug matches: dont-break-the-streak → growth-engine, im-in-the-room → the-craft (5) Write Genius-style annotations using Lenny MCP (mcp__lennysdata__search_content, mcp__lennysdata__read_content) to research the source material. Each track needs 3-5 annotations with lyricText, note, sourceTitle, sourceUrl. Also update concept, keyInsight, sources, keyQuote, quoteSpeaker, storyBehind. DONE: Both tracks updated with new audio, covers, correct albums, and rich annotations. pnpm build passes. DO NOT: create duplicate track entries — update existing ones. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, both MP3s exist in public/audio/, both covers exist in public/covers/.
steps: Extract cover art from both MP3s with ffmpeg
Read lyrics from batch2/lyrics/ markdown files
Research source material via Lenny MCP for both tracks
Update existing track objects in albums.ts with correct albumSlug, new annotations

features.description: BATCH 3 NEW — ask-for-help + nervous-system (The Human Side album): Brand new tracks. For each: (1) Extract cover: ffmpeg -i content/songs/batch3/new-mp3s/[file].mp3 -an -vcodec copy public/covers/[slug].jpg (2) Copy MP3 to public/audio/[slug].mp3 (3) Read lyrics from content/songs/batch3/lyrics/[slug].md (4) Add new Track object to the-human-side tracks array in lib/albums.ts with all fields: slug, title, albumSlug (the-human-side), trackNumber, duration (estimate from MP3 or put '3:30'), audioUrl, coverImage, genre, mood, tags, lyrics (from the md file — full lyrics between the --- markers), concept, keyInsight, sources, keyQuote, quoteSpeaker, storyBehind, annotations (5) Use Lenny MCP (mcp__lennysdata__search_content + mcp__lennysdata__read_content) to research source material for rich annotations. ask-for-help source: 'On asking for help' by Natalie Rothfels. nervous-system source: 'Managing nerves, anxiety, and burnout' by Jonny Miller. Each track needs 3-5 annotations. DONE: Both tracks added, pnpm build passes. DO NOT: modify existing tracks. VERIFY: pnpm build passes, MP3s and covers exist in public/.

FINAL BUILD + TRACK NUMBER CLEANUP: (1) Run pnpm build and fix any TypeScript errors. (2) Verify all 28 new MP3 files exist in public/audio/ with correct slug filenames. (3) Verify all 28 cover images exist in public/covers/ with correct slug filenames. (4) Renumber trackNumber fields within each album so they are sequential (1, 2, 3...) based on the order tracks appear in each array. (5) Verify no duplicate slugs exist across all albums. (6) Run pnpm build one final time. DONE: Clean build, all files in place, sequential track numbers. VERIFY: pnpm build passes with zero errors.
